scope_note: Harvard's official application pages do not publish a minimum GPA cutoff or admitted-student GPA distribution. This panel therefore summarizes what Harvard officially says about courses, grades, and transcript review instead of inventing a GPA threshold.
policy_summary: Harvard does not state a minimum required GPA on its official application-requirements page. Instead, it emphasizes challenging coursework, performance over time, and transcript review in context.

what_mit_does_say.claim: There is no 'one size fits all' rule about which curriculum to study during secondary school years.
Students should challenge themselves by taking courses deemed appropriate by their teachers and counselors.
Applicants to Harvard should excel in a challenging high school math sequence, and Harvard recommends four years of math courses in high school.
Harvard requires a midyear school report so it can review performance in the first half of senior year coursework.
